title: Marketing Consulting Services

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541613 ($34,007,872,680) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541613 ($79,535,987,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $282,178 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541613); class <5: 41,556 firms, avg revenue/firm $395,427 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $61,884 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 3,444 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,727,383 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $270,335 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 1,925 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,595,461 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $562,690 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 1,662 firms, avg revenue/firm $8,903,738 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,393,435 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 274 firms, avg revenue/firm $47,364,223 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,412,501 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 186 firms, avg revenue/firm $120,739,011 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $18,895,655 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 1,936
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Market research synthesis, competitive analysis, positioning decks, campaign performance reporting, and content strategy drafts are heavily AI-exposed. CMO Survey (2026) documents AI now powers 24.2% of all marketing activities — core analyst tasks. Client C-suite persuasion, organizational change judgment, and facilitation of complex stakeholder workshops persist.

breakdown_by_role.role: 11-1021 General and Operations Managers (owner-operators and managing principals)
breakdown_by_role.rationale: In 49,047-firm industry where 41,556 firms have <5 employees, this layer is substantially owner-operators. Client relationships, selling, and business development persist — these are the principal asset. Internal reporting, scheduling, and coordination automate. Fraction slightly higher than 541612 principals because small boutique owners do more admin relative to client advisory.

breakdown_by_role.role: 13-1161 Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
breakdown_by_role.rationale: This is the code's core billable role. Market research synthesis, persona development, competitive analysis, and content strategy are among the most AI-substitutable professional tasks. HubSpot 2025: 35% of marketers use AI primarily for content creation; 30% for data analysis and insights. Judgment on what strategy a client organization will actually execute persists.

Proposal and pitch deck production automates substantially with AI. Relationship selling, referral network management, and closing complex engagements do not. Marketing services are relationship-purchased — the rep's network is the product.

Status reporting, deliverable tracking, scheduling, brief documentation, and campaign timeline management automate substantially. Client expectation management, resolving scope disagreements, and navigating internal client politics persist.
Client intake, brief collection, status communications, and routine Q&A are highly automatable. Sensitive relationship-management situations persist.
Marketing operations documentation, analytics reporting, process optimization analysis, and workflow design have substantial AI exposure. Judgment on complex cross-functional implementation persists.

Martech implementation, analytics platform development, and campaign tracking code are AI-augmented (code generation). Architecture decisions, complex integrations, and data pipeline design remain judgment-heavy.
Bookkeeping, invoicing, media spend reconciliation, financial reporting, and billing management are highly automatable. Licensed attestation and complex financial judgment persist.
Policy documents, job descriptions, onboarding materials, and compliance documentation automate. Employee relations judgment persists.

breakdown_by_role.role: Unlisted tail (writers, designers, brand specialists, digital/content consultants, data analysts, creative directors)
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Unlisted tail = 1.0 minus listed top-10 wage share (0.5273) = 0.4727. For 541613, this tail is dominated by content creators, copywriters, designers, brand consultants, and digital specialists — occupations absent from 4-digit 5416 OEWS but central to 541613. CMO Survey (Jan 2026): AI now powers 24.2% of all marketing activities; marketers project 55.9% in 3 years. HubSpot 2025: 66% of marketers use AI; content creation (35%) and data analysis (30%) are top uses. The creative/content/digital tail has the highest AI exposure in professional services — generative AI creates copy, designs concepts, produces market research syntheses at consultant quality. Fraction 0.55 represents: creative/content work ~65% automatable, data analysis ~50% automatable, brand strategy judgment ~25% automatable, weighted by sub-segment share. Hypothesis clearly labeled: tail composition estimated for this 6-digit code.

pi_dist.rationale: Marketing consulting billing is dominated (~60%) by project fees anchored to estimated hours/day rates — the most direct AI-deflation channel. At contract renewal, client procurement teams benchmark fees against reduced delivery hours as AI tools collapse research and content production time. Forrester (2025) documents holding company revenues fell 1.2% while ad spend grew 8.6% — confirmed fee deflation already occurring at scale. Retainers (~30%) provide some insulation but are renegotiated annually and benchmarked to effort. Value-based/performance fees (~10%) are the only true AI-insulated model. No credential or statutory anchor exists to resist client-side fee pressure. Score 0.35 reflects weak firm pricing control: most efficiency gains flow to clients as lower fees or reduced engagement scopes.
sources: Forrester (via Forbes 2025): marketing agencies in 2026 — holding company revenues fell 1.2% as ad spend grew 8.6%
ctacquisitions.com marketing agency exit guide: fees anchored to day rates/estimated hours, renegotiated at renewal
agencies.co marketing agency valuation 2025: retainer vs project billing dynamics

pi_moat.rationale: Marketing consulting has near-zero statutory protection: no license, no credential, no physical act required. Active client bypass is documented: Forrester forecasts 15% reduction in agency jobs in 2026; CMOs are building in-house AI capabilities; marketing teams deploying AI report 300% ROI. In 41,556 firms with <5 employees (85% of firm count), client relationships are fully portable with the individual consultant — zero institutional switching cost. The only surviving moats are: (1) niche vertical specialization with deep relationships (rare in the majority of boutiques), (2) martech stack ownership creating mild switching costs for implementation clients, and (3) executional integration (media buying where the firm controls placement relationships). These apply to a minority of the code. Score 0.30 reflects the weakest moat position in professional consulting — product substitutability is high, bypass is active, and no credential defends the revenue.
sources: ritnerdigital.com (2026): The Agency Model Is Breaking — clients replacing agencies with AI internal teams
enrichlabs.ai (2026): How to Replace Your Marketing Agency with AI — practitioner-targeted guide signaling market shift
Forbes/Forrester (Oct 2025): Marketing Agencies in 2026 — 15% agency job reduction forecast, agency model under structural pressure

t50_years.derivation: Current adoption: HubSpot 2025 State of Marketing reports 66% of marketers already use AI in their role (74% in US). Marketing consulting practitioners are among the fastest AI adopters in any professional services sector. The 50% firm-level material-adoption threshold has already been crossed based on practitioner data. However, 'material adoption in the automatable functions' at the firm-operator level (i.e., firms systematically deploying AI to execute research, content, and analytics deliverables at production scale) is a higher bar than individual tool use. CMO Survey (Jan 2026) shows AI powers 24.2% of marketing activities, projected to reach 55.9% in 3 years — the 50% milestone is ~2-3 years out on the activity-share curve. Using 2.0 years as central estimate (by mid-2028), reflecting that marketing is the fastest-adopting professional services segment, current practitioner adoption already exceeds 65%, and the gap between individual AI use and firm-level systematic deployment is closing rapidly.

current_adoption_pct.source: HubSpot 2025 State of Marketing (n=1,500+ marketers worldwide, vendor survey), corroborated by CMO Survey Duke Fuqua Jan 2026 (n=308 marketing leaders)
current_adoption_pct.figure_quoted: 66% of marketers already use AI in their role; US adoption at 74%. CMO Survey Jan 2026: AI and ML now powers 24.2% of all marketing activities, nearly doubling from 13.1% in 2024.

historical_analogs.source: CRM/marketing automation adoption curves; HBR analysis of technology adoption pace acceleration (2013 updated with recent data); CMO Survey longitudinal data on technology adoption in marketing
historical_analogs.figure_quoted: Marketing automation (HubSpot, Pardot, Marketo) went from niche to ~50% mid-market adoption in 7-8 years (approx. 2010-2018). Digital marketing tools generally reached 50% in 5-8 years. Technology adoption pace is speeding up with each wave.

owners_60plus_pct.source: U.S. Census Bureau Annual Business Survey 2022 (via ctacquisitions.com Boomer Business Succession Wave Report 2024-2030)
owners_60plus_pct.figure_quoted: 52.3% of US business owners are age 55 or older; 22.8% aged 65 and above (Census ABS 2022). The 60+ cohort estimated at ~37% interpolating between 55-64 (29.5%) and 65+ (22.8%) bands.

succession_shortage_documented.evidence: EPI State of Owner Readiness: only 20-30% of businesses that go to market actually sell; CEPA-per-Boomer-business ratio approximately 1 CEPA per 360 Boomer-owned employer firms; 78% of business owners lack a formal transition team. Marketing agency succession is further complicated by key-person risk (client books portable with founder) noted explicitly by agencies.co and gomerge.com exit planning guides.

active_consolidators.source: Industry M&A coverage: mergersandacquisitions.net, investorideas.com, privsource.com; Accenture Song deal count FY2024-2025
active_consolidators.figure_quoted: PE buyers accounted for 42.4% of deal volume in 1H 2025 (highest in 5 years). Active PE-backed roll-up platforms: Stagwell, Mountaingate Capital, Truelink Capital, New Engen, Power Digital Marketing, Bounteous, Dept, Real Chemistry — estimated 8 platforms actively doing marketing consulting add-ons in the last 24 months. Accenture Song: 46 acquisitions FY2024 but primarily digital agency/IT services overlap.

buy_mult.source: ctacquisitions.com marketing agency business valuation guide (2026); capitala.co marketing agency valuations and EBITDA multiples
buy_mult.figure_quoted: Project-only generalist marketing agencies: 2.0-4.0x EBITDA. Mixed retainer/project: 4.0-6.0x. For marketing consulting boutiques (predominantly project-based, <$5M EBITDA, after EBITDA normalization from SDE): central estimate 4.0x normalized EBITDA.

exit_mult.source: ctacquisitions.com marketing agency valuation guide; capitala.co EBITDA multiples for $5M+ agencies; strategic acquirer disclosed transaction data (Stagwell, Accenture Song)
exit_mult.figure_quoted: Marketing agency $5-10M EBITDA range: 8.1-10.7x depending on specialization. Holding-company tier: 8.0-12.0x. Agencies with 3 years double-digit growth, low concentration, extended client engagements: 8x-12x. Central estimate for scaled marketing consulting platform ($10M+ EBITDA, retainer-heavy, vertical specialty): 9.0x EBITDA.

pricing_structure.value: ~60% project fees (fixed-fee or T&M: strategy decks, research reports, campaign planning, content production, competitive analyses — effort-anchored and renegotiated at renewal); ~30% retainers (ongoing brand/content/strategy advisory, monthly advisory relationships); ~10% value-based or performance-tied fees (conversion-linked, ROI-measured engagements)
pricing_structure.source: nmsconsulting.com consulting fees and pricing 2026; ctacquisitions.com marketing agency exit guide; agencies.co marketing agency valuation
pricing_structure.figure_quoted: Project-based and retainer are the two dominant billing models in consulting; marketing consulting is majority project-based given the deliverable-centric nature of campaigns and strategy assignments. Value-based pricing remains a small minority.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Uplift_pp = V_raw × 100 = 0.208756 × 100 = 20.88pp. A = 5.0 (≥ 5). Check required: uplift 20.88pp >> 3pp threshold — strongly positive, no tension. The large uplift (20.88pp) is consistent with high A (5.0): AI creates very large value (V=8.35) and is being adopted quickly (A=5.0). The C score (1.05) is the binding constraint — clients capture most of the value through fee deflation rather than operators retaining it as margin. Consistent.

terminal_value.justification: Marketing consulting has no license, statute, or physical act protecting demand. The only mechanism is CMO accountability and the value of an outside perspective. Evidence that the product itself is being replaced: (1) Forrester documents holding company revenues fell 1.2% in 2025 even as worldwide ad spend grew 8.6% — direct evidence of engagement volume decline, not just fee compression. (2) Forrester forecasts 15% reduction in agency jobs in 2026. (3) Over 10,000 US marketing jobs eliminated in first 7 months of 2025 due to AI automation. (4) CMO Survey projects AI to power 55.9% of all marketing activities within 3 years (from 24.2% today). (5) The typical 541613 firm produces research, content strategy, competitive analysis, and positioning decks — all of which client-side AI tools (integrated into Salesforce, HubSpot, Adobe) now produce at consultant quality. For the 41,556 firms with <5 employees (85% of firm count) doing generalist strategy and content work, AI is replacing the product. The only surviving demand is niche vertical expertise, facilitated multi-stakeholder workshops, and board-level brand strategy — a fraction of the code. Classification: MELTING. AI is replacing the operator's product, not merely cutting the operator's costs.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: V was derived from HubSpot 2025 State of Marketing practitioner adoption survey and CMO Survey Duke Fuqua Jan 2026 activity data — no M&A deal counts, consolidation activity, or PE transaction data referenced. A was derived from CMO Survey AI activity projection (24.2% today → 55.9% in 3 years) and historical marketing automation adoption analog — no deal activity referenced. Confirmed no proxy use for V or A.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Billing mix: ~60% project fees (T&M/fixed, effort-anchored), ~30% retainers (effort-estimated), ~10% value-based. Project fees are the primary deflation channel: as AI collapses research and content delivery hours (Forrester confirms 300% ROI from AI in marketing, meaning 75% cost reduction), procurement teams renegotiate at renewal. The Forrester evidence of agency revenue declining while ad spend grew confirms fee deflation is occurring industry-wide. Retainers renegotiated annually based on perceived value, which declines when clients can use ChatGPT to do the same work. Reflected in pi_dist = 0.35 (strong client bargaining power, no credential anchor, direct substitutability) and pi_moat = 0.30 (active client bypass, no statutory protection, key-person portability). Together C = 10 × 0.35 × 0.30 = 1.05, the lowest capture score of any consulting sub-sector — appropriately reflecting the maximum deflation exposure.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: t50=1.5 → A=6.67 → S≈4.71; t50=3.5 → A=2.86 → S≈3.81. Range ≈ ±0.45 around central S=4.39. Significant sensitivity given A is the largest positive factor. Note: A cannot exceed 10; capped.
pi_dist=0.25 → C=0.75 → S≈4.10; pi_dist=0.45 → C=1.35 → S≈4.65. Range ≈ ±0.28 around central S. Moderate sensitivity. pi_dist is the key judgment call in the face of strong evidence of deflation.
pi_moat=0.20 → C=0.70 → S≈3.97; pi_moat=0.40 → C=1.40 → S≈4.68. Range ≈ ±0.36. Jointly sensitive with pi_dist through C; the MELTING terminal class and evidence of active bypass support the low end.

reviewer_note: 1. role_mix is 4-digit 5416 proxy (management consulting dominated). For 541613, the true occupational mix skews heavily toward marketing specialists, content creators, designers, and digital specialists — none of which appear in the listed top-10. These constitute the majority of the unlisted tail (0.4727 wage share), which drives most of the ai_replaceable_share (0.260/0.458 = 57% of the total). This is the key uncertainty: if the tail is more brand-strategist heavy (lower fraction ~0.45) vs. content/copywriter heavy (fraction ~0.65), ai_replaceable_share shifts substantially. Estimate labeled as hypothesis. 2. V_raw arithmetic note: stored score uses 0.4559 × 0.457831 = 0.208756 (not 0.208773 as approximated in derivation — slight difference in final digit of ai_replaceable_share sum). The stored value is the exact sum of contributions. 3. The MELTING terminal classification combined with low C (1.05) means this code's S=4.39 is substantially driven by V (8.35), A (5.0), B (7.40), and M (5.0) — four strong dimensions pulled down by a very weak C. A reviewer should confirm whether the MELTING classification is appropriate or whether some demand preservation in vertical niches warrants PRESSURED; the boundary here is close. If PRESSURED, terminal cap would be higher. However, the Forrester evidence of actual revenue declines alongside spend growth supports MELTING. 4. buy_mult = 4.0x is conservative for project-based generalists but may overstate for firms with 60%+ project revenue that look more like freelancer-collectives than institutionalized consulting firms. Some 541613 firms may sell at 2-3x SDE (=1.5-2.5x EBITDA normalized). If buy_mult = 3.0x: M = clamp(4×6/3, 0, 10) = clamp(8.0, 0, 10) = 8.0 → S would rise to ~5.0. The multiple spread is a significant uncertainty. 5. active_consolidators = 8 is an estimate based on named PE platforms; many straddle NAICS 541810 (advertising agencies) and 541613 and the boundary is porous. Coded LOW quality accordingly.
